title: Multi-phase flow visualizations based on fluid occupation time
abstract: Systems, methods, and computer program products can be used for visualizing the behavior of flow of two or more fluid phases, wherein a fluid phase behavior is represented in a visualization. One of the methods includes determining an occupation time, which is the amount of elapsed time from when a fluid phase first occupies a particular location until a second time. The method includes generating data for a visualization, with a location in the visualization corresponding to the particular location, and with the generated data for that location in the visualization indicating the occupation time.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method performed by a data processing system for visualizing the behavior of flow of two or more fluid phases, wherein a fluid phase behavior is represented in a visualization, the method comprising: determining an occupation time, which is the amount of elapsed time from when a fluid phase first occupies a particular location until a second time; and generating data for a visualization, with a location in the visualization corresponding to the particular location, and with the generated data for that location in the visualization indicating the occupation time. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the visualization of the fluid phase includes assigning a color based on the occupation time. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: at a third time subsequent to the second time, updating the occupation time to be the difference between the third time and the first, and updating the visualization based on the update occupation time. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein locations in the visualizations associated with smaller amounts of occupation time, relative to other amounts of occupation time of other locations in the visualization, represent a displacement front. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the fluid phase is a displacing phase, wherein locations with decreased amounts of occupation time, relative to other amounts of occupation time of other locations, represent a displacement front, and wherein the method further comprises: generating data for rendering a visualization of the displacement front. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein a fluid phase is a water based or gas based fluid phase. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the second time comprises a current time. 8 The method of claim 1, wherein the location is a location within or around a porous medium. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the porous medium is a rock. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein one of the two or more fluid phases represents a an oil based fluid phase. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the location is specified in a data set or in an image. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising: simulating, in a simulation space, flow of two or more fluid phases in a physical media; wherein the visualization is a visualization of simulation results. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the location in the visualization is a voxel, wherein each voxel in the visualization is colored, and wherein a color of a particular voxel is set according to the occupation time. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising rendering the visualization. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the visualization is included in a video or in a set of visualizations. 16. The method of claim 1, wherein the location in the visualization represents a surface location.
